what_it_does: A single React custom hook, useAppController, that creates and returns a large controller object containing state, refs, and editor-bound handlers for a tldraw-based canvas application. It is meant to be created once inside a component that has useEditor and exposed via React context so presentational region components can read shared state and actions without prop drilling. The file holds graph state, UI indication/locus logic, chat/voice/proposal handling, settings, cognition event folding, fleet/corpus/annotation mirrors, and SSE stream management.
observations: Holds roughly 37 useState declarations plus many useRef guards in one hook.
Uses refs (indicatedRef, indicateModeRef, wtBusyRef, journeyIdRef, sessionRef, settingsOpenRef, cognitionTurnRef, etc.) to avoid stale closures in event handlers and SSE callbacks.
Implements seq-based event merging (mergeEvents) to deduplicate SSE/poll events and cap history at 200 items.
Folds cognition.* SSE events into a live turn frame (foldCognition) with latent/firing/ran/failed/injected role statuses.
Mirrors registry data (fleet, corpus, node states, mode directives, roles, voice paths/status, cognition info) into React state with fail-loud error surfaces.
Contains voice lifecycle including push-to-talk, auto-listen, barge-in, stream cancellation, Web Audio scheduling, and WAV playback.
Implements proposal/offer consent flow with approve, steer, defer, dismiss, revive, and set-aside handlers.
Provides address-scoped helpers: address help, history, self-changes, freshness, versions, annotations.
Manages UI indication (indicate), journey recording/replay, and mobile bottom-sheet tab state.
Boot sequence loads object info, UI registry, capabilities, model lists, and cognition projection, collecting boot errors into a visible notice.
standouts: The sheer scope of the hook: it centralizes what was a 1070-line Hud local component into a single state container.
Ref-based synchronous concurrency guards (wtBusyRef, indicateModeRef) to prevent double-fire before React re-render.
foldCognition: a pure, additive event folder that builds a live cognition turn from backend lifecycle events without owning cognition state.
Barge-in architecture: persistent analyser RMS during playback, voice reader cancellation, play source tracking, and epoch gating.
Explicit fail-loud pattern: errors are surfaced in state (fleetErr, corpusErr, addressHelpError, notice) rather than swallowed.
conventions: React functional hook pattern with useState/useRef/useEffect.
TypeScript with many any-typed payloads for API/registry data.
Deterministic code_id comments and feature tags (F0, U4, B1, L-fe, etc.) inline.
Async/await with try/catch for API calls; fire-and-forget void for non-blocking refreshes.
Registry-is-truth: frontend mirrors backend-provided lists and never hardcodes model/state catalogs.
Ref mirrors state when stale closures matter (settingsOpenRef, sessionRef, cognitionTurnRef, reviewMockupRef, indicatedRef).
concerns: Monolithic hook with hundreds of lines of state and handlers may be difficult to unit test and reason about in isolation.
Heavy use of any reduces type safety and compile-time guarantees for API contracts.
File is truncated in the provided view, so full effect cleanup, dependency arrays, and some handler bodies are not visible.
Many state setters and refs in one closure could trigger unnecessary re-renders or complicate memoization for context consumers.
Window global assignment (window as any).__editor = editor for automation/debugging.
notes: This file is the carved-out central nervous system of the canvas UI: a single hook that owns all former Hud-local state and exposes it via context, with extensive inline feature tagging and ref-based concurrency guards.
questions: What is the exact shape of the AppController type and where is it consumed?
How are the useEffect subscriptions and audio/recorder resources cleaned up when the editor/hook unmounts?
Which API endpoints are guaranteed stable, given the many any-typed payloads?
How does the hook handle rapid successive calls to doRun or sendChat beyond the ref guards?
What determines whether a proposal is interactive vs a simple B1 offer card?
How is the context provider wired in App.tsx, and do all region components use React.memo to avoid rerenders?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments describe it as a mechanical extraction of the former 1070-line Hud into a single state container, and the code is indeed one large hook holding state, refs, and handlers for context consumption.
apparent_themes: event-driven projection (SSE/poll events folded into view state)
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to turn a sprawling UI monolith into a single, deterministic, context-readable controller while preserving exact behavior, adding fail-loud error surfaces, and projecting backend event streams into React state without the frontend owning authoritative data.
novelty: The combination of deterministic symbol IDs, dense inline feature tagging, a pure cognition event folder, and an explicit barge-in/stream-cancel voice architecture within one React hook is unusual.
contribution: It provides a consolidated controller surface that packages editor state, graph operations, UI locus logic, chat/voice/proposal handling, settings, cognition projection, and SSE event management into one context-readable object, reducing prop-drilling and giving region components a single place to read shared behavior.
summary_for_embedding: useAppController.ts is a large React custom hook that centralizes canvas application state, editor handlers, SSE event folding, chat/voice/proposal/settings/cognition logic, and exposes the resulting controller via context for presentational region components.
intention_for_embedding: Extract the former Hud monolith into a single reusable state container so region components can read shared state and actions without prop drilling.

symbols.does: Creates and returns the central app controller hook.
symbols.description: Initializes all state, refs, and editor-bound handlers for the canvas application and returns a controller object meant to be provided via React context to presentational region components.

symbols.does: Sets the mockup under review and its studio locus.
symbols.description: Updates reviewMockup state/ref, records the optional ui:// address via indicate(), and stores any pointed element content so the RHM can ground chat on the mockup or a specific unregistered element.

symbols.does: Fetches the gallery corpus from the backend.
symbols.description: Calls api.corpus(), surfaces errors in corpusErr, and writes the returned item list into corpus state.

symbols.does: Loads the comment thread for a ui:// address.
symbols.description: Calls api.annotations(addr), tolerates list or wrapped shapes, and stores the address-keyed thread; fail-loud on error.

symbols.does: Type alias for a proposal choice.
symbols.description: Describes one option in a rich consent offer, including verb, address, args, label, and distinguishing summary.

symbols.does: Merges events by sequence into a capped list.
symbols.description: Deduplicates incoming events against existing ones using seq, sorts newest-first, and caps at 200 to keep keys stable.

symbols.does: Folds one cognition event into the live turn frame.
symbols.description: Pure, additive reducer that updates role statuses (latent/firing/ran/failed/injected), counts injected characters, and closes the turn on turn.done.

symbols.does: Ensures a role entry exists in the cognition frame.
symbols.description: Helper inside foldCognition that creates a default latent role record before updating it.

symbols.does: Refreshes now/events/inbox/last-change/panels.
symbols.description: Calls several API endpoints and merges events; surfaces a visible notice if the refresh fails so stale data is not silent.

symbols.does: Reloads live model lists per kind.
symbols.description: Fetches chat and embed model registries independently, captures each kind's error, and marks the fleet as loaded.

symbols.does: Opens a course-of-action decision compiler result.
symbols.description: Calls api.coa(id) and stores the framing/code surface for review.

symbols.does: Mirrors graph node config into a ref and tick.
symbols.description: Builds a per-node config map, compares it to the current ref, and bumps configTick only when changed to avoid remounting the inspector form.

symbols.does: Opens the SSE event stream.
symbols.description: Creates an EventSource to receive backend lifecycle events and dispatches them into state; exact dispatch logic is truncated in the view.

symbols.does: Reloads graph, edges, config, stuck nodes, and polls.
symbols.description: Calls loadGraph, syncs config, repaints stuck status, polls surfaces, and triggers maybeReact.

symbols.does: Fits the tldraw camera to the graph.
symbols.description: Camera/layout helper that adjusts the editor view to show the loaded graph.

symbols.does: Measures DOM element width.
symbols.description: QuerySelector helper that returns the bounding width of a selected element for layout calculations.

symbols.does: Measures DOM element height.
symbols.description: QuerySelector helper that returns the bounding height of a selected element for layout calculations.

symbols.does: Watch-and-react backend-gated behavior.
symbols.description: Likely triggers automated reactions/comments depending on mode; details truncated in the provided view.

symbols.does: Adds a node of a given type to the graph.
symbols.description: Creates a new node via the backend and refreshes the canvas.

symbols.does: Connects the currently selected nodes.
symbols.description: Wires selected shapes together through the connect API.

symbols.does: Commits a wire connection between two nodes/ports.
symbols.description: Calls the backend connect endpoint and refreshes the graph after a successful wire.

symbols.does: Writes a single config key for a node.
symbols.description: Posts a node config update and bumps the inspector tick.

symbols.does: Surfaces a node's output for inspection/building.
symbols.description: Fetches and displays or acts on a node's stored output.

symbols.does: Initiates a build from a node's output.
symbols.description: Creates a build intent from the given node and output value.

symbols.does: Deletes selected canvas nodes.
symbols.description: Removes selected shapes/nodes from the graph via the API.

symbols.does: Removes the persistent UI-indicated DOM class.
symbols.description: Cleans up the visual indication marker from the previously indicated element.

symbols.does: Determines click behavior for an address.
symbols.description: Returns whether clicking an element should annotate or operate based on its registry mapping.

symbols.does: Sets the indicated ui:// locus.
symbols.description: Updates indicated state/ref, applies the persistent DOM class, records journey steps if recording, and triggers address-scoped helpers.

symbols.does: Toggles click-to-indicate on or off.
symbols.description: Flips both the React state and the synchronous ref so the document click handler sees the new mode immediately.

symbols.does: Appends an address to the active journey record.
symbols.description: Called while journey recording is on to save each indicated address as a navigation step.

symbols.does: Posts a comment at the indicated locus.
symbols.description: Calls api.annotate with the indicated address and text, then refreshes annotations.

symbols.does: Runs the studio generate step for a mockup.
symbols.description: Dispatches a plan-mode generation request and stores the proposed result.

symbols.does: Creates a build intent from operator text.
symbols.description: Turns a natural-language request into a structured build intent for the backend.

symbols.does: Toggles the pointed-element registry marker.
symbols.description: Marks or unmarks a ui:// element as the pointed locus.

symbols.does: Clears the pointed-element registry marker.
symbols.description: Removes the currently pointed element locus.

symbols.does: Pointer down handler for canvas interactions.
symbols.description: Low-level DOM pointer event handler used by the drag-to-wire or long-press interaction layer.

symbols.does: Pointer move handler for canvas interactions.
symbols.description: Low-level DOM pointer event handler used during drag interactions.

symbols.does: Pointer up handler for canvas interactions.
symbols.description: Cleans up timers after a pointer interaction ends.

symbols.does: Suppresses the iOS long-press context menu.
symbols.description: Prevents the default context menu when a long-press has already fired.

symbols.does: Global document click handler for UI indication.
symbols.description: Resolves clicked elements to ui:// addresses, respects modal exclusions, and calls indicate() when indicate mode is on.

symbols.does: Loads the help bundle for an indicated address.
symbols.description: Fetches what_this_is/how_to_use/how_to_change plus presentation preferences and surfaces errors.

symbols.does: Posts a learned presentation preference for an address.
symbols.description: Sends a preference kind/arg to the backend and refreshes address help.

symbols.does: Loads addressed event history.
symbols.description: Fetches the trajectory of events at a ui:// address for the History region.

symbols.does: Loads self-change audit for an address.
symbols.description: Fetches code-scope filtered changes and stale/note metadata for the indicated element.

symbols.does: Reverts a self-change by sha.
symbols.description: Calls api.revert for the given change and refreshes the audit.

symbols.does: Sends an operator chat turn.
symbols.description: Builds focus (selected nodes, indicated address, mockup, pointed element) and dispatches to the chat or streaming chat endpoint.

symbols.does: Applies a chat response to state.
symbols.description: Processes returned history/proposal/actions and updates chat/proposal state accordingly.

symbols.does: Applies backend action reactions after a chat turn.
symbols.description: Executes follow-on actions emitted by the RHM response.

symbols.does: Approves a pending proposal/option.
symbols.description: Calls api.act for the chosen offer, resolves revived inbox items, and clears the proposal.

symbols.does: Steers a proposal with operator text.
symbols.description: Sends a refinement back to the RHM to re-offer.

symbols.does: Defers a proposal to the inbox.
symbols.description: Queues the current offer as a live escalation for later resolution.

symbols.does: Dismisses a proposal without queuing.
symbols.description: Clears the proposal and shows a notice that it was set aside.

symbols.does: Dismisses the current proposal.
symbols.description: Rejects/ignores the pending offer and clears proposal state.

symbols.does: Revives a deferred offer from the inbox.
symbols.description: Loads an escalation back into the proposal surface for approval/dismissal.

symbols.does: Changes the active brain mode.
symbols.description: Dispatches a mode change to the backend and updates local mode state.

symbols.does: Starts a guided walkthrough on a topic.
symbols.description: Initiates a walkthrough/guide session.

symbols.does: Switches the active persona.
symbols.description: Calls the persona switch endpoint and updates local persona state.

symbols.does: Loads registries for the settings surface.
symbols.description: Fetches roles, voice paths/status, mode registry, and composition config when settings open.

symbols.does: Opens the consolidated settings modal.
symbols.description: Sets settingsOpen and optionally a tab, then loads settings data.

symbols.does: Writes a single RHM config slot.
symbols.description: Posts a config update via set_rhm_config and refreshes local cfg.

symbols.does: Refreshes the cognition run index.
symbols.description: Fetches recent cognition runs for the settings cognition section.

symbols.does: Runs a cognition role.
symbols.description: Dispatches a role run against the cognition engine and captures busy/error/result state.

symbols.does: Creates a new cognition role.
symbols.description: Posts a role spec to the cognition engine and updates result state.

symbols.does: Cycles the canvas layer view filter.
symbols.description: Toggles between all/origin-only/system-only layer views.

symbols.does: Portals the selected node's output.
symbols.description: Surfaces/opens the stored output of the currently selected node.

symbols.does: Resolves a UI target string to a view action.
symbols.description: The single sink that drives the view to any ui:// or node address via DOM spotlight or canvas drive.

symbols.does: Flashes a transient spotlight on a DOM element.
symbols.description: Applies the ui-spotlight class to the element identified by domKey/infoKey and removes it after a timeout.

symbols.does: Navigates the canvas to a node.
symbols.description: Selects and frames the given node in the tldraw editor.

symbols.does: Starts a review walk over item ids.
symbols.description: Creates a review/walkthrough session for the given items.

symbols.does: Refreshes a walkthrough session.
symbols.description: Reloads the current review session state from the backend.

symbols.does: Ends the active walkthrough.
symbols.description: Closes the review session and clears related state.

symbols.does: Records an operator response for the current walk step.
symbols.description: Submits approve/reject/comment for the active walkthrough step.

symbols.does: Advances the walkthrough to the next step.
symbols.description: Requests the next item in the review session.

symbols.does: Fetches stale/fresh verdict for a selected node.
symbols.description: Derives whether a node's cached output is stale relative to current inputs.

symbols.does: Computes the versioned address for a selection.
symbols.description: Helper that returns the stored-output address of the selected node, if any.

symbols.does: Fetches prior versions of an addressed output.
symbols.description: Loads the ref-versions trail for a selected node's output address.

symbols.does: Runs the graph or selected nodes.
symbols.description: Executes the computation pipeline, clears runError in finally, and updates stuck nodes from events.

symbols.does: Updates stuck node set from run events.
symbols.description: Reads stuck node ids from event/result data and stores them in stuckNodes ref.

symbols.does: Applies stuck status to shapes.
symbols.description: Propagates the stuck node set onto the tldraw shapes after loadGraph.

symbols.does: Dispatches a generic action to the backend.
symbols.description: General action dispatcher used by various UI flows.

symbols.does: Unlocks/resumes the Web Audio context.
symbols.description: Called on user gesture so subsequent voice replies can play on iOS and locked browsers.

symbols.does: Schedules a WAV buffer for playback.
symbols.description: Decodes and plays a WAV chunk through the shared AudioContext, respecting the current play epoch.

symbols.does: Stops active voice playback.
symbols.description: Cancels the voice stream reader and stops all live AudioBufferSourceNodes.

symbols.does: Streams TTS for a text reply.
symbols.description: Fetches /api/voice/stream and feeds chunks into playWavBuffer.

symbols.does: Converts a base64 string to an ArrayBuffer.
symbols.description: Utility for decoding base64 WAV data from the voice stream.

symbols.does: Runs a complete voice turn from audio blob to reply.
symbols.description: Sends STT audio to the backend, handles the response, and optionally speaks it back.

symbols.does: Handles operator speech during playback.
symbols.description: Cancels the current voice reply and playback so the operator can interrupt.

symbols.does: Toggles push-to-talk voice recording.
symbols.description: Starts or stops a MediaRecorder for a single voice utterance.

symbols.does: Stops the hands-free auto-listen session.
symbols.description: Ends the continuous listen loop and releases the recorder/stream.

symbols.does: Starts the hands-free auto-listen session.
symbols.description: Begins the continuous listen loop that auto-finalizes utterances and re-arms with a fresh recorder.

symbols.does: Reads microphone RMS for voice activity detection.
symbols.description: Measures current input volume from the analyser to detect speech.

symbols.does: Auto-listen timing tick.
symbols.description: Schedules the next step of the continuous voice listen loop.

symbols.does: Monitors for barge-in during playback.
symbols.description: Watches RMS while speaking and triggers bargeIn when sustained speech is detected.

symbols.does: Sets the voice input mode.
symbols.description: Switches between push-to-talk and hands-free voice modes.

symbols.does: Applies RHM configuration updates.
symbols.description: Posts RHM updates to the backend and refreshes local state.

symbols.does: Sets a single brain configuration knob.
symbols.description: Updates a brain/engine setting via the API.

symbols.does: Sets model context/service value.
symbols.description: Adjusts per-service model context allocation.

symbols.does: Refreshes the voice/brain fit report.
symbols.description: Recomputes budget fit for the current selection of model, base_url, persona, and TTS engine.

symbols.does: Starts a voice pipeline service.
symbols.description: Brings up the requested STT/TTS service.

symbols.does: Selects a chat model row.
symbols.description: Picks a model from the detailed chat-model picker and updates config.

symbols.does: Refreshes the conversation thread list.

symbols.does: Starts a new conversation thread.
symbols.description: Creates/resets the current thread and clears thread-bound state.

symbols.does: Opens an existing conversation thread.

symbols.does: Toggles conversation recording.
symbols.description: Starts or stops recording the current conversation session.

symbols.does: Starts a debrief session.

symbols.does: Enables or disables voice output.

symbols.does: Handles microphone button press.
symbols.description: Gesture handler that primes audio and toggles recording.

symbols.does: Toggles reverse journey recording.

symbols.does: Replays a recorded journey.
